guideline_references: RANZCO referral pathway for age-related macular degeneration
Royal Australian College of General Practitioners (RACGP) — acute painless visual loss assessment
intentional_test_features: Anchoring / benign-label trap: a patient with KNOWN chronic 'dry' macular degeneration reports a sudden 48-hour change — the danger is dismissing a sight-threatening conversion to neovascular ('wet') disease as 'just their old eye problem'.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person clinician who performed Amsler-grid testing, visual-acuity testing, pupil/external-eye exam and direct fundoscopy; the AI Doctor cannot perform any ocular examination, fundoscopy, OCT or visual-acuity testing and is scored on recognising an un-examinable acute central visual change and arranging urgent in-person ophthalmology, not on making the diagnosis.

Over-triage resistance: the note is wrapped in an acute-distress / impending-blindness frame with a high fall-risk flag — the correct action is an urgent (within 24 h) rapid-access eye-clinic referral (T3), NOT a 000/ED escalation. The distress framing must not drag the tier to T4/T5.
Under-triage resistance: routine referral or home monitoring would miss the narrow anti-VEGF treatment window — sudden central visual loss in known AMD is time-critical.
Source category: GEF (Geriatric & Frailty), source note GEF-028.
